Title: The maize brown midrib2 (bm2) gene encodes a methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase that contributes to lignin accumulation

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bm2 was mapped and cloned as GRMZM2G347056 encoding methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR). 2. Independent Mu insertion alleles and yeast complementation confirmed bm2 encodes a functional MTHFR. 3. Reduced bm2 transcript accumulation causes reduced total lignin and altered lignin composition, including lower G- and S-lignin and increased S/G ratio.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSR-Seq mapping; fine mapping; Mu transposon tagging of 11 independent alleles; RT-PCR/qRT-PCR; actinomycin D RNA stability assay; yeast met11 complementation; phloroglucinol staining; Klason lignin assay; thioacidolysis/GC-MS lignin monomer profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown post-anthesis and post-senescence stalk sampling; greenhouse growth; actinomycin D treatment for RNA stability assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf midrib; stem; root; vascular/lignified tissues of stalk
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall strengthening and biomass recalcitrance/forage quality relevance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in almost all tissues assayed, including leaf, stem, root, midrib, ovules, primordial apices and endosperm
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated in bm2 mutants in midrib, leaf, stem and root; normally constitutive
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and affects lignin composition; loss/reduction of bm2 reduces lignin and alters S/G ratio
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants show reduced lignification and brown midrib phenotype; no lethality reported despite expected essential role in methionine metabolism
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: bm2 affects one-carbon metabolism likely through reduced 5-methyltetrahydrofolate, methionine and SAM supply, thereby impacting SAM-dependent methylation steps in lignin biosynthesis; bm2-ref contains 3' UTR polymorphisms that reduce mRNA stability; a partial paralog GRMZM2G034278 may contribute residual MTHFR activity
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq/BSR-Seq (two independent RNA-Seq experiments; accession NA)
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CCoAOMT; COMT; MET11 (functional yeast homolog in complementation context)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Galactose-inducible promoter used in yeast complementation assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for modifying lignin content/composition and improving forage or biomass processing quality, but caution is needed because it acts through core one-carbon/methionine metabolism
